title: Uncloneable registration of an internet of things (iot) device in a network
abstract: An IoT device has a public device identifier and a private device identifier, where the public device identifier is publicly available and the private device identifier is secret but kept in a secure device database as a correspondence. A registration request is sent from the IoT device to an association server in communication with the device database having an association between IoT public identifier and a corresponding IoT private identifier. The association server which receives the registration request responds with a registration acknowledgement containing, in encrypted form, the private device identifier of the original request and, optionally, the public device identifier associated with the registration request. The requesting IoT device receives the association acknowledgement, decrypts the private device identifier, compares it to its own device identifier, and if they match, sends one or more association requests.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1) An authentication system operative to register an IoT device to a network, the authentication system comprising: an IoT device having a public device identifier, a private device identifier, and an association request generator which is enabled after receiving an authentication containing a private device identifier from an authentication server which matches the IoT device private device identifier; an authentication server operative to receive registration requests from an IoT device, the authentication server forwarding the registration request to an association server, the authentication server, upon receipt of a registration acknowledgement for an IoT device, forwarding the registration acknowledgement to the IoT device; an association server having a device database with an association between an IoT public device identifier and an IoT private device identifier, the association server also having an optional gateway database with an association between a gateway identifier and one or more IoT public device identifiers, and optionally a user database with an 

association between a username, a password, and a gateway identifier; the IoT device sending a registration request to the authentication server, the authentication server forwarding the registration request to the association server, the association server responding with an encrypted registration acknowledgement containing at least the public device identifier of the registration request and the associated private device identifier, the authentication server forwarding the encrypted registration acknowledgement to the IoT device making the registration request, the IoT device comparing the private device identifier in the registration acknowledgement with its own IoT private device identifier, and if they match, making a WLAN association request. 2) The IoT device of claim 1 where the IoT public device identifier is a media access controller (MAC) address. 3) The IoT device of claim 1 where the gateway identifier is a gateway public IP address or a gateway MAC address. 

 4) The IoT device of claim 1 where the IoT device public identifier present is at least one of: a visible MAC address, a QR code, or a bar code. 5) The IoT device of claim 1 where at least one of the forwarded registration request, or the private device identifier is encrypted. 6) A method for registration of an IoT device having a public device identifier and a private device identifier, the method comprising: the IoT device sending a registration request containing the public device identifier to an authentication server; the authentication server forwarding the registration request to an association server, the association server having access to a device database of unique associations between public device identifiers and private device identifiers for each device, the association server responding with a registration acknowledgement containing a public device identifier and a private device identifier in encrypted form; the IoT device decrypting the registration acknowledgement and extracting the public device identifier 

and also the private device identifier, the IoT device comparing the extracted private device identifier with its internal private device identifier to form a match result, the IoT device transmitting an association request if the match result is true. 7 ) The registration method of claim 6 where the IoT device sending and the IoT device receiving is using a WLAN protocol, a Bluetooth protocol, or a ZigBee protocol. 8) The registration method of claim 7 where the WLAN protocol is 802.11. 9) The registration method of claim 6 where the IoT public device identifier is a MAC address. 10) The registration method of claim 6 where the registration request received by the association server includes a gateway ID as well as the IoT public device identifier, the association server comparing the gateway ID and the IoT public device identifier of the registration request with the gateway identifier associated with the IoT public device identifier in a gateway database, and if the 

registration request IoT public device identifier is associated with the gateway identifier in the gateway database, responding with an encrypted registration acknowledgement containing the IoT public device identifier and associated IoT private device identifier from the association server device database. 11) A method for registration of an IoT device having a public device_ID and a private device ID, the method comprising: the IoT device issuing a registration request using the public device_ID; after receiving an encrypted registration acknowledgement which includes an encrypted received public device_ID and an encrypted received private device_ID, the IoT device: decrypting a received private device_ID and a received public device_ID; if the received public device ID matches the IoT public device_ID and the received public device_ID matches the IoT private device_ID, issuing an association request. 12) The method of claim 11 where the encrypted registration acknowledgement includes an encrypted received 

private device_ID from an association server which maintains a database containing a private_ID associated with a public_ID. 13) The method of claim 12 where a user action causes the database to associate a private_ID to a public_ID and optionally also to a particular gateway which forwarded the registration request to the association server. 14) The method of claim 13 where the user action includes using information printed or displayed on the IoT device, including at least one of a bar code, QR code, or MAC address. 15) An association server for IoT registration, the association server having a user/password database, the association server receiving IoT device database registration requests, gateway database registration requests, and gateway requests; upon receipt of an IoT device database registration request which includes a public device_ID and a private device_ID accompanied by a username and password, when the username and password authenticate from the user/password 

database, placing an entry into a database which associates the private device_ID with the public device_ID; upon receipt of a gateway registration request accompanied by a gateway identifier and a public device_ID, returning a registration acknowledgement containing the private device_ID associated with the public device_ID from the device database when the gateway identifier and the public device_ID are both present in the gateway database. 16) The association server of claim 15 where the gateway registration request is encrypted. 17) The association server of claim 15 where the registration acknowledgement is encrypted.
